metadata.title: Claude, On Empty
metadata.color_semantics: claude: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream
metadata.greeting_note: hello lexicon: Zulu. Wagwan check: charsum('claude-on-empty') % 10 == 2, not 0.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk, never a brand card. SPARK-LINE LAW: inner beats pair the spark with one short serif line; typing is B00-only. OUTRO LAW: outro restates the title. Spine: B00 ASK · B01–B03 the problem (stall, where it goes, the mistake) · B04–B05 the fix (route by task, author high run low) · B06 payoff (breathing beat, no callout) · B07 VERDICT (artifact view: the routing on one page) · B08 title-restate outro. Premise from Bear: people stop working for days when Fable 5 runs out; selective routing to Opus keeps Fable available when it matters. HANDOFF LAW: second-to-last beat = composer with a paste-ready prompt for the viewer (greeting 'Your turn.'); typing appears in cold open and handoff only.
metadata.description_blurb: Running out of Claude isn't a limit problem — it's a routing problem. Five moves that keep Fable available when it matters.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: I know people who stop working for days because they ran out of Claude. Not because the work stopped — because the model did. That's not a limit problem. That's a routing problem.

props.command: why am I out of usage until Friday?

beats.narration_text: The real cost isn't tokens. Run dry on Tuesday and the work just waits for the reset. The wait is the price — and it's self-inflicted.
Where did it go? One chip: the flagship, effort on High, for everything. Hard problems, email drafts, renaming files — all billed at genius rates.
Flagship-by-default feels safe. But you're spending judgment on chores. The model that can hold your whole architecture in its head does not need to rename your files.
Route by task. The bulk work — running skills, batch renders, long refactors — hand it to Opus in Claude Code. That lane is built for the long haul.
Save Fable for the words that get reused: the skill, the plan, the tricky call. Author with your best model once — the quality freezes into the file, and every cheap run inherits it.
Then Thursday's hard problem arrives — and Fable is still there. Same budget. Different week.
The routing, on one page. Fable writes what gets reused. Opus runs it. Medium effort for the everyday, High only when it earns it. Usage is a budget — spend it where only your best will do.

artifactLines: Fable 5 — skills, plans, the tricky calls. Words that get reused.
Opus + Claude Code — runs, batches, refactors. The long haul.
Effort High — only when the problem earns it.

beats.narration_text: Want your own routing table? Your turn. Take this prompt — Claude will build it from your actual week.

props.command: look at how I use you this week and suggest a routing: which tasks deserve the flagship, which don't? AskUserQuestion.

beats.narration_text: If this saves your Thursday, subscribe. Nik Bear Brown.

props.title: Claude, on empty.
props.subline: subscribe · spend it where it counts
